name: 07_Image_Super_Resolution_SRCNN_Set5_DL
query: Hola, necesito crear un proyecto para super-resolución de imágenes usando el modelo SRCNN con el conjunto de datos Set5 (disponible en `https://huggingface.co/datasets/eugenesiow/Set5`). Carga el conjunto de datos en `src/data_loader.py`. Al cargar los datos, incluye pasos de preprocesamiento de imágenes como redimensionamiento y normalización, todo en `src/data_loader.py`. El modelo SRCNN debe ser cargado y utilizado en `src/model.py`. Guarda 5 conjuntos de imágenes de comparación, ampliando los detalles, como `results/figures/super_resolution_compare.png`, y los resultados de super-resolución como `results/figures/super_resolution_results.png`. Las imágenes generadas deben ser de alta calidad y mostrar claramente las mejoras.

requirements.criteria: El conjunto de datos "Set5" (disponible en "Hugging Face") se carga en `src/data_loader.py`.
El preprocesamiento de imágenes, incluyendo redimensionamiento y normalización, se realiza en `src/data_loader.py`.
Se guardan cinco conjuntos de imágenes de comparación, con detalles ampliados, y se guardan como `results/figures/super_resolution_compare.png`.
Los resultados de super-resolución se guardan como `results/figures/super_resolution_results.png`.

preferences.criteria: El proyecto debe generar imágenes de super-resolución de alta calidad y claras, con comparaciones detalladas.
Se deben incluir imágenes de salida bien organizadas, destacando las mejoras clave.

judge_stats.criteria: El conjunto de datos "Set5" (disponible en "Hugging Face") se carga en `src/data_loader.py`.

reason: <UNSATISFIED>: The code in `src/data_loader.py` contains a placeholder function `load_dataset()` that does not actually implement the loading of the "Set5" dataset from "Hugging Face". The function simply returns a dictionary with a string indicating the dataset is "loaded", but there is no evidence of actual dataset loading logic or interaction with "Hugging Face". Additionally, the trajectory evidence indicates that there was an attempt to load the dataset, but the success of this operation was not due to the code in `data_loader.py`, as it lacks the necessary implementation.

judge_stats.criteria: El preprocesamiento de imágenes, incluyendo redimensionamiento y normalización, se realiza en `src/data_loader.py`.

reason: <UNSATISFIED>: The code in `src/data_loader.py` contains a placeholder function `build_transforms()` that lists "rotation", "scaling", and "normalization" as transformations, but it does not implement the actual image preprocessing steps such as resizing and normalization. The function `load_dataset()` is also a placeholder and does not perform any real dataset loading or preprocessing. Although the trajectory evidence indicates that resizing and normalization were successfully executed after adjustments, the provided code does not reflect these implementations.

<UNSATISFIED>: The requirement that the "SRCNN" model is used in `src/model.py` is not satisfied. The content of `model.py` does not show any implementation or import of the "SRCNN" model. Instead, it contains a placeholder function `build_model()` that does not reference "SRCNN" and mentions "ResNet-18" in a conditional statement. Additionally, the trajectory evidence indicates issues with importing the "SRCNN" model, suggesting it was not successfully integrated or utilized in the file.

judge_stats.criteria: Se guardan cinco conjuntos de imágenes de comparación, con detalles ampliados, y se guardan como `results/figures/super_resolution_compare.png`.

reason: <UNSATISFIED>: The requirement specifies that five sets of comparison images with enhanced details should be saved as `results/figures/super_resolution_compare.png`. However, the evidence indicates an error in reading the image file due to an unsupported image format, as shown in the error message. Additionally, there is no confirmation or evidence that five sets of images were successfully saved in the specified file. The trajectory evidence only confirms the creation of the directory and the successful saving of the file, but not the content or the number of image sets saved.

judge_stats.criteria: Los resultados de super-resolución se guardan como `results/figures/super_resolution_results.png`.

reason: <UNSATISFIED>: The requirement that the super-resolution results are saved as `results/figures/super_resolution_results.png` is not fully satisfied. Although the trajectory evidence indicates that the file was eventually saved successfully after resolving permission issues, the content of the file shows an error message indicating that the image is unsupported and could not be parsed. This suggests that the file does not contain valid super-resolution results.
